purpose: Directory-only contract for the initial automation-engine repo shape. Specific files are intentionally omitted.
rules: autonomy/ is the stable automation kernel: interfaces, data contracts, reusable primitives, stage contracts, and generic runners.
implementations/ mirrors the autonomy/ hierarchy and contains concrete code that satisfies stable contracts.
Plugin is a loading and activation mode, not the top-level source-code category.
autonomy/ should not import implementations/ directly; CLI, runtime, or deploy code should load implementations by config/spec.
deploy/targets/ contains source-controlled remote target bundles.
runtime/ contains generated local state and should be reproducible from source plus CLI commands.
lab/ contains unstable experiments, captured runs, and analysis artifacts.
User-facing workflows should be reachable through ./cli/automa rather than duplicated scripts.

root.responsibility: Local source of truth for automation engine code, concrete implementations, deployment bundles, generated runs, and CLI access.

children.responsibility: Stable core package for contracts, primitives, stage shapes, and orchestration. It should not contain concrete vehicle adapters, perception algorithms, or operation recipes.
Vehicle-agnostic interface for sensor reads, pulses, actions, capabilities, and adapter boundaries.
Stable perception contracts and shared primitives. Active algorithms live in implementations/perception/.
Frame observations and low-level image comparisons with no scene-specific policy.
Reusable feature tracking primitives.
Reusable relative-motion and motion-group primitives.
Reusable floor and traversability primitives.
Reusable landmark and distance primitives.
Mapper loaders and stable mapper entry points that assemble configured implementations.
Generic observe, memory, pattern, projection, action, and snapshot cycle with optional stages.
Generic runtime contracts for loading engines, stepping them, applying mode gates, reporting status, and hosting controller loops.
Concrete code that satisfies autonomy contracts. This tree intentionally mirrors autonomy/ so inspection and promotion paths stay obvious.
Concrete vehicle adapters implementing autonomy/vehicle contracts.
PiRacer-specific HTTP, DonkeyCar, and hardware assumptions.
Metrics UI / Chase simulator adapter exposing only vehicle-like I/O to autonomy code.
Concrete perception mappers and plugin-style sensor-to-evidence algorithms.
Concrete non-default decision engines and stage implementations. The stable idle default belongs to autonomy/runtime/.
Concrete runtime hosts for environments that need special integration code.
DonkeyCar part and drive-loop integration.
Concrete bounded operation plans and workflows.
Action-and-capture checks to confirm commands register.
Source-controlled deploy bundles for physical or remote runtimes.
One subdirectory per remote target family.
Physical PiRacer deployment bundle, including app resources, vendor source material, and local patches.
Files synced to the car's app/runtime directory.
Generated pinned DonkeyCar checkout recreated from the source-controlled vendor manifest and patches.
Local changes applied to vendor or target code during deploy preparation.
Primary command surface. Common user workflows should live here instead of duplicated scripts.
Vehicle, simulator, deploy, implementation, runtime, and diagnostic command implementation.
Common scenario tests, with offline defaults and explicit live-simulator coverage.
Generated and mutable local state, reproducible from source plus CLI commands.
Local runtime directories keyed by discovered or configured vehicle id.
One local runtime area for a simulator or physical vehicle.
Generated controller bundle with core code, concrete implementations, releases, active configuration, and worker state.
Extracted stable autonomy package from the active local controller release.
Extracted concrete implementations from the active local controller release.
Hashed controller archives and manifests available for activation or remote deployment.
Perception and decision activation manifests plus automation worker state and rolling output.
Target-specific activation metadata staged for physical deployment.
Short-lived temporary output created by CLI or local processing.
Unstable self-contained experimentation space that can churn without changing core architecture.
Experiment code, configs, notes, and artifacts kept together for review or later extraction.
Captured images, reports, overlays, and one-off generated analysis output.
Local UI source that belongs to this project. Simulator UI stays in Metrics UI.
Frontend files specific to the DonkeyCar/PiRacer control surface.
Human-readable architecture docs, diagrams, backlog summaries, decision notes, and stable contracts.
Low-level helpers that are not user workflows. Anything overlapping with ./cli/automa should be removed or folded into CLI services.
Calibration and measurement scripts not yet promoted into operation implementations.
Perception inspection helpers not yet promoted into CLI.
Remote helper scripts called by CLI deploy flows.
